part1.context: राम राम जी, मैं Stage App से Neha बोल रही हूँ , आज हम आपकी किस तरह मदद
part1.explain: The user said “mam” · the ASR wrote “line”. A single wrong word changes the meaning the model learns; catching these is exactly what golden transcripts fix.

part1.context: Hi, I am Mira, Am I Talking to Sonam?
part1.explain: The user said “here” · the ASR wrote “है”. A single wrong word changes the meaning the model learns; catching these is exactly what golden transcripts fix.

part1.context: Hello! This is Neha from the derma co. Would you prefer to speak in En
part1.explain: The user said “okay” · the ASR wrote “ok”. A single wrong word changes the meaning the model learns; catching these is exactly what golden transcripts fix.

part1.context: Hello, मैं Neha बोल रही हूं Pronto Company की तरफ़ से.
part1.heard: यह screening center कहां पड़ता है
part1.golden: यह screening center कहां पड़ता है
part1.explain: The user said “screening” · the ASR wrote “scanning”. A single wrong word changes the meaning the model learns; catching these is exactly what golden transcripts fix.

part1.context: Hello Deepak Kumar Vijay मैं नेहा बोल रही हूँ Paytm से
part1.explain: The ASR got this one right. Confirming a clean turn matters as much as catching an error · false alarms poison the dataset.

part2.setup: A field-support agent is registering a cooler complaint. Listen from 01:10 · the user reads out the barcode.
part2.explain: The agent captured the wrong barcode. The expert logged: “Wrong bar code captured” · everything downstream (the service ticket) breaks silently.

part2.setup: Same support flow, different call. Listen from 03:40 · the user says their store name.
part2.explain: The user clearly said the store name; the agent never captured it. Expert note: “the user mentioned their store name but wasn't captured”.

part2.setup: Listen from 00:21 · the user makes a request right at the start.
part2.explain: The user asked to continue “hindi mein” · the agent didn't register the request at all. Expert note: “did not capture hindi mein”.

part2.setup: A lead-qualification call. Listen from 00:12 to how the user speaks and how the bot answers.
part2.explain: The user speaks Hindi throughout; the bot keeps replying in English. Expert note: “user speaking in hindi · bot replying in english”.

part2.setup: Listen from 00:30 · the user states a language preference.
part2.explain: The user wanted Tamil; the bot continued in English. A stated language preference is an instruction, not a suggestion.
